pattern: The versions form a monotone false…false-true…true predicate, so binary search on the answer finds the boundary with logarithmically few oracle calls.
hints: Checking versions one by one calls the API up to n times; the predicate never flips back once it turns bad.
Keep a lo/hi range that always brackets the first bad version and probe its midpoint.
A bad midpoint moves hi onto it, a good one moves lo past it; when the range collapses you have the answer. Watch for overflow when averaging in other languages.

edge_inputs.description: only the last version is bad
boundary early in a longer range
near the 32-bit ceiling — a naive (lo+hi) overflow trap upstream

stress.description: random n with a random first bad version inside it
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1000000000)))
    bad = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [n, bad]
